description_long: This object is a short, straight-bladed sword or large dagger with a double-edged, tapering blade that appears metallic and dark in color. The blade is symmetrical and gradually narrows to a pointed tip, with a subtle central ridge running along its length. The crossguard is simple, straight, and slightly angled downward, providing basic hand protection. The handle is cylindrical, metallic, and appears to have a subtle grip pattern, ending in a flat, hexagonal pommel. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The finish is dark and slightly glossy, suggesting a steel or iron construction.
description: A short, double-edged, straight-bladed sword or dagger with a dark metallic finish, simple crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a flat pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blade and handle, emphasizing the blade's sharpness and symmetry.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the blade, crossguard, and handle visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, metallic, flat hexagonal pommel, subtle grip pattern
